conclusions: ? tiles are strong, but pure ? greed was not the best family.
Purple+ chest before ? was the best practical EV profile in this aggregate.
Chest-first had the best 7-chest completion rate, but lower average value.
Upgrade-lean was weak as a primary normal-board plan.

strategies.label: Purple Before ?
strategies.title: Purple Before ?
strategies.cardSummary: Prioritizes purple+ chest value before chasing ? variance. Best practical EV in the aggregate.
strategies.profile: Best practical value profile from the 80,000-run family aggregate. It treats ? tiles as strong, but not stronger than known purple+ chest value.
strategies.useWhen: Use when you want the best tested average value without going full ?-greedy.
strategies.avoidWhen: Avoid if your goal is raw 7-chest completion rate instead of value.
notes: Normal
Best average value in the aggregate; won 7 of 10 batches.
Tradeoff
Lower 7-chest rate than chest-first, but much better average value.

strategies.label: Balanced
strategies.title: Balanced Current
strategies.cardSummary: Close to the top profile and safer as a general default. Strong baseline for future tests.
strategies.profile: The current balanced scoring model. It was close to the best EV result and remains a stable default for mixed boards.
strategies.useWhen: Use when you want a conservative default that avoids overfitting to one test profile.
strategies.avoidWhen: Avoid if you specifically want the best measured EV or max chest-count completion.
notes: Normal
Second-best average value; close enough to remain a valid default.
Testing
Useful as the baseline for future A/B comparisons.

strategies.label: ? After Chest
strategies.title: Question After Chest
strategies.cardSummary: Takes more chest count before leaning into ?. Better completion rate than balanced, lower EV than Purple Before ?.
strategies.profile: Hybrid profile that establishes chest count before weighting ? tiles harder. It sits between value routing and completion routing.
strategies.useWhen: Use when you want more chest count without committing to full chest-first.
strategies.avoidWhen: Avoid when pure average value is the only goal.
notes: Normal
Higher 7-chest rate than Purple Before ? and Balanced.
Tradeoff
Lower average value than the top EV profile.

strategies.label: Chest First
strategies.title: Chest First Completion
strategies.cardSummary: Best chest count and 7-chest completion rate, but loses average value.
strategies.profile: Quantity-first profile. It collects more chests and completes 7 chests most often, but gives up average value.
strategies.useWhen: Use when your goal is completion rate, chest quantity, or Undetermined Fate ticket volume.
strategies.avoidWhen: Avoid when average chest value is more important than count.
notes: Normal
Best 7-chest completion rate in the aggregate.
Tradeoff
Much lower average value than Purple Before ?.

strategies.label: ? Greedy
strategies.title: Question Greedy
strategies.cardSummary: Confirms ? tiles are strong, but pure ? greed underperforms the better hybrid profiles.
strategies.profile: Aggressive ?-tile profile. Useful as a test control showing that ? is strong but should not override known value every time.
strategies.avoidWhen: Avoid as a main recommendation; it underperformed the top hybrid profiles.
notes: Normal
Takes the most ? tiles among the main profiles.
Finding
Pure ? greed was not the best strategy family.

strategies.label: Upgrade Lean
strategies.title: Upgrade Lean Control
strategies.cardSummary: Upgrade-heavy routing was the weakest family in this test. Keep as a control/niche option.
strategies.profile: Upgrade-heavy control profile. It overvalues upgrade-card routing relative to known chest value and ?-hybrid play.
strategies.useWhen: Use only as a control or for unusual boards with dense upgrade targets.
notes: Normal
Lowest average value among tested families.
Finding
Upgrade cards are useful, but upgrade-lean routing is not a strong primary plan.
